channel.title: NYT > Style
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Everyone Thinks Parisians Rule Fashion. Everyone Is Wrong.
item.description: How the Antwerp Six shaped the modern fashion world, and why everyone is still obsessed.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.category: Royal Academy of Fine Arts (Antwerp, Belgium)

item.title: Most Stylish Stars of the 2026 Awards Season: Teyana Taylor, Odessa A’Zion and More
item.description: Whether loud or quiet, formal or relaxed, the awards season attire of these eight individuals was surprising, delightful and worn with ease.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi

item.title: Trends From Toyko Fashion Week 2026: Layermaxxing, Power Clashing and More
item.description: Power clashing, extreme layering and dressing daintily were potent themes on and off runways at Tokyo Fashion Week.
item.dc:creator: Simbarashe Cha

item.title: Can You Fake Being Rich?
item.description: A spate of popular social media accounts offer advice on how to achieve an “old-money” look.
item.dc:creator: Guy Trebay
item.media:description: The appearance of wealth, and how to get it, has become a hot topic on social media.

item.title: How the French National Football Team Outdressed the N.B.A.
item.description: The French National Football team draws global attention with their eclectic style.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:credit: Ewen Gavet/Icon Sport, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Hugo Ekitike in the attention-winning outfit of the week.

item.title: The Artists Embroidering Custom Clothes for Zohran Mamdani, Josh Safdie and Bowen Yang
item.description: Amid a fast-fashion landscape in which so much can be easily produced, some consumers long for something special — merchandise personalized by human hands.
item.dc:creator: Jason Diamond

item.title: Carole Radziwill on FX’s ‘Love Story’ and Returning to ‘Real Housewives’
item.description: Her deceased loved ones are characters on a hit TV show, her name is in the Epstein files and she’s returning to “Real Housewives.” What does she make of it all?
item.dc:creator: Iva Dixit
item.category: The Real Housewives of New York (TV Program)
Love Story: John F Kennedy Jr & Carolyn Bessette (TV Program)
item.media:credit: Lucia Bell-Epstein for The New York Times
item.media:description: Rebutting others’ accounts of her deceased loved ones — her husband, Anthony Radziwill; his cousin, John F. Kennedy, Jr.; and Carolyn Bessette Kennedy — has long been something of a duty for Carole Radziwill. But even as she has readily taken up that cause, she is also tired of doing it.

item.title: Why the Artemis II Astronauts Will Be Wearing Orange
item.description: It’s not just any orange. It’s International Orange.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:description: The Artemis II crew, in an orange officially known as AMS Standard 595 color #FS 12197.

item.title: Mayor Mamdani Attended a Hip Seder. Some Guests Weren’t Thrilled.
item.description: The mayor spoke at Michael Dorf’s annual Passover celebration, taking hecklers and a comic’s last-minute cancellation in stride.
item.dc:creator: Jesse McKinley
item.media:credit: Dolly Faibyshev for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani and Brad Lander, the former city comptroller, at City Winery on Monday evening.

item.title: A New Jersey Teen Finds Treasure, and More, in Abandoned Storage Units
item.description: Michael Haskell, 17, set out to make some money from his locker dives. He ended up learning about life.
item.dc:creator: Alex Vadukul and Vincent Alban
item.media:description: Driving back home to Bergen County, N.J., with the latest haul.

item.title: Street Style Look of the Week: Vintage Military Clothing
item.description: Attire spotted outside in Tokyo had a past life as military gear.
item.dc:creator: Simbarashe Cha

item.title: Lola Rykiel Is Selling Women the Pleasure of Clothes
item.description: Lola Rykiel, the granddaughter of Sonia Rykiel, creates clothes for women who don’t want to look like anyone else.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Iredale
item.media:credit: Jonas Unger for The New York Times

item.title: On ‘Ill-Advised,’ Bill Nighy Finds His Funniest Role Yet: Podcaster
item.description: On his podcast “Ill-Advised By Bill Nighy,” the English actor counsels listeners on etiquette and fashion. His objective is to not make anything worse.
item.dc:creator: Ariel Leve
item.media:description: Bill Nighy described his award-winning podcast, “Ill-Advised With Bill Nighy,” as a refuge for “the clumsy and awkward” and an invitation to “squander time.”

item.title: Why Barbie Dream Fest in Florida Is Drawing Comparisons to Fyre Festival
item.description: Fans had high hopes for Barbie Dream Fest, a Mattel-sanctioned gathering in Fort Lauderdale. But in a cavernous convention center, the dream seemed to fizzle.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:description: The bright pink world of Barbie struggled to fill the Broward County Convention Center. “It felt like being just trapped in a big, dark warehouse,” one attendee said.

item.title: Agosto Machado Is Dead; Artist Memorialized New York’s Avant-Garde
item.description: An experimental theater veteran, he collected the ephemera of his friends and colleagues. As they began to die, he made shrines honoring them.
item.dc:creator: Penelope Green

item.title: ‘I Think That MAGA Is Dying:’ Inside the Youth Movement at CPAC
item.description: At a sparsely attended Conservative Political Action Conference, young Republicans were eager to start the post-Trump era.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Taylor Pemberton
item.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: The annual Conservative Political Action Conference has been gradually waning in energy for years, but empty convention halls and an anemic speakers list gave many young people in attendance a doomed feeling.

item.title: Travel Influencers Share Destinations So Good That They Returned
item.description: Even travel influencers, who spend their lives on the go, have their hearts stolen by certain places.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Gomelsky

item.title: The Plus Size Park Hoppers Are Kind of Famous at Disney World
item.description: Five influencer friends have amassed half a million online followers with videos that help larger people find comfy chairs, ride roller coasters and feel confident.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher and Mary Beth Koeth
item.category: Disney, Walt, World (Lake Buena Vista, Fla)
item.media:description: The Plus Size Park Hoppers visit Disney parks as often as their work schedules and budgets will allow, usually about six times a year.

item.title: In Borneo, Malaysia, Experience Rawness and Luxury
item.description: Experiencing the rawness of nature, but with an emphasis on luxury, deep in the ancient rainforests of Borneo and where Langkawi meets the Andaman Sea.
item.dc:creator: David Belcher
item.category: Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas

item.title: Why Marriage, for So Many, Is Less Appealing Than Ever
item.description: From Gen Z to Gen X, a pause in the march to the altar, or a decision to skip it altogether, is becoming more common.
item.dc:creator: Tammy LaGorce

item.title: These Nuns Podcast, Pray and Play Ultimate Frisbee, and Viewers Can’t Get Enough
item.description: They pray, they play Ultimate Frisbee and they have unwittingly become a meme.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher and Rosem Morton
item.media:description: Dominican Sisters in Michigan have found internet fame with a new podcast where they talk about everything from converting to Catholicism to “Saturday Night Live.”

item.title: Yearning for Sun, New Yorkers Soak Up the Sauna
item.description: After a stretch of cold weather, the Culture of Bathe-ing Festival’s waterfront gathering brought out the swimsuits and a different kind of chill.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr and Vidhya Nagarajan

item.title: Letizia Mowinckel, Who Procured Jacqueline Kennedy’s Pink Chanel Suit, Dies at 105
item.description: Mrs. Kennedy was wearing the suit when her husband was assassinated in Dallas in November 1963, and it became soaked in his blood.
item.dc:creator: Ash Wu
item.media:credit: Cecil Stoughton, White House Photographs/GHI, via Universal History Archive, Getty Images
item.media:description: President John F. Kennedy and first lady Jacqueline Kennedy descending the stairs from Air Force One at Love Field in Dallas on Nov. 22, 1963. The president was assassinated about an hour later.

item.title: How ‘Love Story’ Gave Viewers a History Lesson on the Kennedys and ’90s NYC
item.description: The FX show, which dramatizes the relationship between John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette, has inspired a legion of younger viewers to dig into the couple’s past and 1990s New York City.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez
item.category: Love Story: John F Kennedy Jr & Carolyn Bessette (TV Program)

item.title: After 26 Weddings, They Finally Had Their Own
item.description: Carolyn Holtzman and Nikhil Jyothinagaram blended Hindu rituals with New Orleans’s signature celebrations.
item.dc:creator: Donna Kim

item.title: They Planned a Wedding in 90 Days
item.description: A boba shop meet-cute and the creation of a fashion business led Nica Yusay and Quang Che to the altar after nearly five years of engagement.
item.dc:creator: Donna Kim

item.title: She Was Sure About the Man. Not So Much About the Dress.
item.description: Natasha Barrow went viral for having doubts about her wedding dress. By the time she and Perry Holen married, she was done with regrets.
item.dc:creator: Tammy LaGorce
item.media:description: Perry Holen and Natasha Barrow celebrated their wedding on March 21 in Ketchum, Idaho. The bride had posted doubts about her Vivienne Westwood dress on TikTok in September but, in the end, loved it.

item.title: Four Years After I Had Last Been Touched, I Wrote Myself Back to Life
item.description: He was 14 years younger and a skateboarder. Why not invite him in?
item.dc:creator: Angela Cravens

item.title: What Is the ‘Gen Z Pout’?
item.description: Their lips are pursed. Their eyes gaze into the middle distance. Is this a real thing?
item.dc:creator: Callie Holtermann
item.media:description: The “Gen Z pout” has been attributed to several young celebrities, including Lily-Rose Depp, who appear to purse their lips with forensic precision when posing for pictures.

item.title: My Girlfriend Texted Her Ex Secretly. Can I Trust Her?
item.description: Our Ask the Therapist columnist, Lori Gottlieb, advises a reader who is feeling conflicted about his partner’s loyalty.
item.dc:creator: Lori Gottlieb

item.title: The Man Who Keeps Time at Madrid’s Royal Palace
item.description: José Antonio Gismera watches over some 190 antique clocks at the palace, winding them weekly and caring for them when they are “sick.”
item.dc:creator: Chantel Tattoli and Emilio Parra Doiztua
item.media:description: The Porcelain Cabinet room at the Royal Palace of Madrid. On display in the center of the room is “Atlas,” an intricate clock that, along with the time, displays the months and signs of the zodiac.

item.title: Leonid Radvinsky, 43, Dies; Built the Adult-Entertainment Giant OnlyFans
item.description: By leveraging social media and the influencer economy, he turned his website into a byword for online pornography in the 21st century.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen

item.title: Tiny Love Stories: ‘Six Years Younger and 30 Pounds Lighter’
item.description: Modern Love in miniature, featuring reader-submitted stories of no more than 100 words.

item.title: What Is ‘One-Sided Monogamy’?
item.description: “Inside the Manosphere,” a new Netflix documentary, has fueled discussions about the relationship style.
item.dc:creator: Gina Cherelus
item.media:description: In a recent documentary by Louis Theroux, left, the manosphere influencer Justin Waller, right, describes his relationship as “one-sided monogamy.”

item.title: What to Know About the ‘Laguna Beach’ Reunion
item.description: The show, which depicted the lives of a group of wealthy high schoolers and helped to pioneer a genre of soap-style reality TV, will return in April with a reunion special.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Roy

item.title: The Good List: 8 Things to Add Delight to Your Day
item.description: March Madness madness, better small talk and the morality of sandwiches.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Kirsch
item.media:credit: Illustration by John Gall. Flowers: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times

item.title: Daphne Selfe, World’s Oldest Professional Model, Dies at 97
item.description: Starting in 1949, she had an on-and-off career that lasted for 75 years. But her biggest success came late in life, modeling for labels like Dolce & Gabbana.
item.dc:creator: Bonnie Wertheim
item.media:credit: Visual China Group, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Daphne Selfe in 2010. Her skin-care and diet regimen, she said, included “Nivea, broccoli and the odd glass of champagne.”

item.title: Our Youngest Child Has Cut Ties With Our Family. Help!
item.description: A reader is distraught over the rift with her 37-year-old child, who is insisting on having a neutral party present for any potential meeting with the rest of the family.
item.dc:creator: Philip Galanes

item.title: BioFluff Is Producing a Faux Fur Made From Plants
item.description: BioFluff, a fashion-tech company based in Paris and New York, is producing a new alternative to animal pelts: one that’s made from plants, not plastics.
item.dc:creator: Chantel Tattoli and Clara Vannucci
